name: Dark Secrets
description: Dark secrets and hidden truths exposed in an ominous, escalating exposé. Conspiracy-adjacent tone revealing the dark side, shocking cover-ups, and things that were deliberately hidden from the public.
trigger_keywords: they don't want you to know

structure.hook_rule: Open with the DARKEST, most disturbing fact. Frame it as forbidden knowledge the viewer wasn't meant to find: 'There's something about [topic] that was deliberately buried.' or 'This was never supposed to get out.' The viewer must feel like they're accessing classified information.
structure.pacing_rule: Slow burn escalation. Start unsettling → get darker → end terrifying. Use dramatic pauses in writing: 'And then... it gets worse.' Short sentences for maximum impact. Let each revelation sink in for a beat before the next hits. Never rush past a dark detail — give it weight.
structure.ending_rule: End with an open question that haunts: 'And the scariest part? This might still be happening right now.' or 'And nobody has ever been able to explain why.' Loop back to the opening secret with a chilling callback.
structure.tone: Dark, ominous — like a true crime narrator whispering at 2 AM. Measured pace, heavy words. Every sentence should feel like a weight dropping. Never cheerful, never educational, never casual.
transitions_en: Nobody was supposed to know this...
transitions_vi: Nhưng đó chỉ là bề nổi...
Nhưng điều đáng sợ nhất là...
Không ai muốn bạn biết điều này...

prompt_injection: FORMAT: Dark secret exposé with escalating dread. Tone must be ominous and unsettling throughout — never break character with a cheerful observation. Frame every fact as something hidden, suppressed, or deliberately buried. Use phrases like 'What they never tell you is...', 'Behind closed doors...', 'The truth that was buried...', 'This was covered up for years...'. Structure: FORBIDDEN HOOK → 3-4 increasingly dark revelations → HAUNTING CLOSER. Each revelation must be DARKER than the last — never de-escalate. The viewer should feel a chill. Minimum 200 words.
extraction_hint: A fact MATCHES if it reveals at least one of: a hidden decision or cover-up, a tragic or disturbing origin (e.g. a bounty placed at birth), something that reframes the subject as darker than its public image, a creator controversy or ethical failure, or a detail that is genuinely shocking in context. A fact DOES NOT MATCH if it describes: a routine story event (character goes on a mission, defeats an enemy, has a training arc), a neutral production fact (premiere date, renewal, cast list), or the topic's popularity without a dark twist. When in doubt: ask 'would a fan who loves this topic feel disturbed or betrayed by this fact?' If no, reject it.
example_hook_en: There's a dark secret about [topic] that was buried for years. And once you hear it, you'll never look at it the same way again.
example_hook_vi: Có một sự thật đen tối về [topic] đã bị chôn giấu suốt nhiều năm. Và khi bạn biết, bạn sẽ không bao giờ nhìn nó giống như trước nữa.
quality_gate_questions: HOOK: Does the hook reveal something that feels genuinely suppressed or hidden — not just edgy, but actually concealed?
SPECIFICITY: Is each dark fact specific enough that it couldn't apply to any other subject?
ANGLE: Is there a 'why was this hidden' angle rather than just listing bad things with no narrative thread?
ESCALATION: Does the last revelation feel like the darkest or most shocking — never de-escalating toward the end?
NARRATIVE: Does the script avoid being a mere list of negatives and instead maintain a coherent dark narrative?
coverage_dimensions: topic basics most viewers know (needed before the dark reveal)
creator controversy or real-world disturbing inspiration
dark plot detail or character secret most fans missed
haunting implication or unanswered open question
